revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Verse transliteration-translation/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23692667944|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|template:Verse transliteration-translation/styles.css}} == Usage == {{tlx|Verse transliteration-translation|2=lang={{var|[[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code}}|3=originalHeading={{var|Heading for Original Poem}}|4={{var|original text}}|5=transliterationHeading={{var|Heading for transliteration}}|6={{var|transliteration}}|7=translationHeading={{var|Heading for translation}}|8={{var|translation}}|9=rtl={{var|yes, if original poem should run right to left}}|10=width={{var|Width of all contents}}}} {{tl|Verse transliteration-translation}} produces a three-column layout meant to contain the original text of a given poem, an English transliteration, and an English translation, in that order, with optional headings. Language and transliteration data is automatically added in accordance with the {{param|lang}}, and the columns break into a vertical layout in viewports below 900 pixels in width. By setting rtl = yes, the leftmost column can be made to run right to left. === Example (the koinobori song by Miyako Kondō)=== {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=ja |originalHeading = Original Japanese |屋根より　高い　鯉のぼり 大きい　真鯉は　お父さん 小さい　緋鯉は　子供たち 面白そうに　泳いでる |transliterationHeading = Rōmaji |yane yori takai koinobori ōkii magoi wa otōsan chiisai higoi wa kodomo-tachi omoshirosō ni oyoideru |translationHeading = English translation |Higher than the roof-tops are the koinobori The large carp is the father The smaller carp are the children They seem to be having fun swimming.}} === Right to left example ([[:en:Chad Gadya|Chad Gadya]]) === {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} produces {{verse transliteration-translation |lang=he |rtl=yes |חַד גַּדְיָא, חַד גַּדְיָא וְאָתָא הַקָּדוֹשׁ בָּרוּךְ הוּא וְשָׁחַט לְמַלְאַךְ הַמָּוֶת ,דְּשָׁחַט לְשׁוֹחֵט דְּשָׁחַט לְתוֹרָא, דְּשָׁתָה לְמַיָּא דְּכָבָה לְנוּרָא, דְּשָׂרַף לְחוּטְרָא דְּהִכָּה לְכַלְבָּא ,דְּנָשַׁךְ לְשׁוּנְרָא, דְּאָכְלָה לְגַדְיָא דְּזַבִּין אַבָּא בִּתְרֵי זוּזֵי |Chad gadya, chad gadya, ve-ata ha-Kadosh Baruch Hu ve-shachat le-mal'akh ha-mavet de-shachat le-tora, de-shatah le-maya de-khavah le-nura, de-saraf le-chutra de-hikkah le-khalba, de-nashakh le-shunra, de-akhlah le-gadya dizabin abba bitrei zuzei. |One little goat, one little goat: Then came The Holy One, Blessed be He, and smote the angel of death, who slew the slaughterer, who killed the ox, that drank the water, that put out the fire, that burned the stick, that beat the dog, that bit the cat, that ate the goat, Which my father bought for two zuzim.}} ==Notes== * For a two-column layout, without a separate transliteration, please use {{tl|verse translation}}. * Please do not use this template for long sections of prose. Whether or not doing so works at present, any such usage may complicate future changes to the template. * If you want to use non-English text in any of the headings, remember to enclose it in a {{tl|lang}} template. * With headings, the text beneath the hedings will be indented, while the headings themselves will not be. Without headings, the entire template will be indented. Additional indentation can be achieved with {{tl|block indent}} ==See also== {{Quotation templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[category:translation templates]] [[category:quotation templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
